---
title: "Publications"
---

This page is ready for papers, preprints, posters, and software notes. The most useful publication pages do two things at once: they provide a complete record, and they give each entry a short summary that explains why it matters.

## Recommended entry format

:::{.stack-grid}
::: {.stack-card}
### Citation

Author list. Year. Title. Journal or venue. Links to DOI, preprint, repository, slides, and data when available.
:::
::: {.stack-card}
### Plain-language summary

One or two sentences that answer: what question did this output address, and what changed because of it?
:::
::: {.stack-card}
### Tags

Use a few stable tags such as theme, method, or dataset so visitors can scan your work without reading everything.
:::
:::

## Sections to maintain

- Peer-reviewed articles
- Preprints and manuscripts in review
- Conference posters and talks
- Software, protocols, and datasets

::: {.note-box}
Once you add real entries, keep the newest outputs near the top and use short summaries instead of long abstracts.
:::
